message:
feat(core,parser,layout,render-svg,render-term): add sequence lifecycle markers, DottedCross arrow, notes, and fragments

Sequence lifecycle events:
- Add LayoutSequenceLifecycleMarker infrastructure with Destroy kind
- Track lifeline start/end Y coordinates for create/destroy events
- Truncate lifelines for created/destroyed participants
- Render destroy markers (X shape) across SVG, terminal cell, and
  terminal canvas backends

New arrow type:
- Add DottedCross arrow variant; fix --x mapping to DottedCross in
  parser (was incorrectly mapped)
- Add cross/dotted-cross handling in terminal renderer

Sequence configuration:
- Parse showSequenceNumbers directive for autonumber enablement
- Add set_init_sequence_show_sequence_numbers() builder method

SVG rendering additions:
- Render sequence notes as rounded yellow boxes
- Render sequence fragments (loop/alt/par/opt/critical/break) with
  dashed borders and kind labels

FEATURE_PARITY.md:
- Comprehensive rewrite documenting layout algorithms, rendering
  surfaces, gaps vs FrankenTUI, and areas where frankenmermaid leads

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-9cy0","title":"Implement class generics, stereotypes, and annotations","description":"# Class Generics, Stereotypes, and Annotations

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
classDiagram
  class Shape {
    <<interface>>
    +draw()
  }
  class List~T~ {
    +add(T item)
  }
  class Color {
    <<enumeration>>
    RED
    GREEN
    BLUE
  }
  note for Shape "Base interface for all shapes"
\`\`\`
- \`<<interface>>\`, \`<<abstract>>\`, \`<<enumeration>>\`, \`<<service>>\` stereotypes
- \`~T~\` generic type parameters on class names
- \`note for ClassName "text"\` annotations
- \`note "general note"\`

## Current State
- Stereotypes: NOT parsed (<<...>> lines within class blocks are ignored)
- Generics: NOT parsed (tildes treated as part of class name)
- Notes: NOT parsed

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Detect <<stereotype>> lines at top of class block. Parse Class~T~ as class name with generic params. Parse \`note for\` and \`note\` statements.
2. **IR**: Add stereotype: Option<String> and generics: Vec<String> to IrNode for class diagram nodes. Add notes to IR.
3. **SVG Renderer**: Show stereotype in guillemets above class name in header compartment. Show generic params after class name. Notes as yellow sticky-note boxes with connection lines.
4. **Tests**: Interface stereotype, generic class, enumeration, notes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:44:44.823305009Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T17:04:33.747294734Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T17:04:33.746791040Z","close_reason":"Implemented class generics (~T~ syntax parsing), stereotypes (already worked), and three-compartment rendering across all 3 renderers. Parser extracts generic params from class~T~ syntax. IrClassNodeMeta.generics stores them. SVG/terminal/canvas display as ClassName<T> in class header.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["class","parity","parser","render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9cy0","depends_on_id":"bd-y2qs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:30.577275181Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":150,"issue_id":"bd-9cy0","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:41Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-aknv","title":"Parse and render sequence diagram activation/deactivation","description":"# Sequence Activation/Deactivation

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
sequenceDiagram
  Alice->>+Bob: Request
  Bob-->>-Alice: Response
  activate Bob
  Bob->>Carol: Forward
  deactivate Bob
\`\`\`
- \`+\` after arrow activates target participant
- \`-\` after arrow deactivates target participant
- \`activate Name\` / \`deactivate Name\` as standalone statements
- Multiple nested activations create stacked boxes
- Activation boxes are narrow rectangles on the lifeline showing active periods

## Current State
- NOT parsed. The +/- modifiers on arrows are ignored.
- No SequenceStatement variant for activate/deactivate.
- Layout does not compute activation box regions.
- SVG renderer does not draw activation boxes.

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Add SequenceStatement::Activate(String) and Deactivate(String). Parse \`activate Name\` and \`deactivate Name\` lines. Also parse +/- suffix on arrow operators.
2. **IR**: Add activation tracking. Option: store as a list of (participant_index, start_edge_index, end_edge_index) tuples in a new IrSequenceMeta struct on MermaidDiagramIr.
3. **Layout**: Compute activation box bounds: x = participant center ± half_width, y_start = message y where activated, y_end = message y where deactivated. Handle nested activations with x-offset.
4. **SVG Renderer**: Draw activation boxes as narrow filled rectangles on lifelines.
5. **Tests**: Simple activate/deactivate, nested activations, +/- syntax on arrows.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:44:09.455081051Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:11:18.670511964Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T02:11:18.669867805Z","close_reason":"Implemented sequence activation/deactivation parsing. Supports both standalone 'activate Name'/'deactivate Name' statements and +/- suffix on arrow operators (Alice->>+Bob). Nested activations tracked via per-participant stacks in IrBuilder. Open activations auto-closed at end of diagram. 4 parser tests added. All quality gates pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","parser","render","sequence"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-aknv","depends_on_id":"bd-6zd9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:24.106138908Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":144,"issue_id":"bd-aknv","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:40Z"},{"id":172,"issue_id":"bd-aknv","author":"ubuntu","text":"ADDITIONAL: Also add the missing async message arrow type -) (open arrowhead) to SEQUENCE_OPERATORS. mermaid-js supports: ->>, -->>, ->, -->, -x, --x, -) (async). Currently only 6 of 7 are implemented.","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:31:25Z"},{"id":173,"issue_id":"bd-aknv","author":"ubuntu","text":"NOTE: The +/- activation modifiers appear AFTER the arrow operator, e.g. 'Alice->>+Bob'. The parser must detect these suffixed on the target participant name, not on the operator itself. This means modifying the sequence message AST parser at line 3851 to strip trailing +/- from target before node lookup.","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:31:25Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-b6gg","title":"Implement accDescription and accTitle accessibility directives","description":"# Accessibility Directives

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
accTitle: Process Flow
accDescr: This diagram shows the order processing pipeline from receipt to delivery.
accDescr {
  Multi-line description
  of the diagram's purpose
}
\`\`\`
- accTitle: sets the accessible title for screen readers (maps to SVG <title> element)
- accDescr: sets the accessible description (maps to SVG <desc> element)
- Both are global, apply to the entire diagram
- Used by all diagram types

## Current State
- accTitle/accDescr are NOT parsed
- SVG renderer already has accessibility support (a11y) but doesn't read these from IR

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Recognize accTitle and accDescr (single-line and multi-line block) directives. Store in MermaidDiagramMeta.
2. **IR**: Add accessible_title: Option<String> and accessible_description: Option<String> to MermaidDiagramMeta.
3. **SVG Renderer**: Emit <title> and <desc> elements within the root <svg> element.
4. **Tests**: Single-line accTitle, accDescr, multi-line accDescr block, SVG output verification.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:32:12.842946048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:24.994240852Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["a11y","parity","parser","render"],"comments":[{"id":174,"issue_id":"bd-b6gg","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:32:22Z"},{"id":194,"issue_id":"bd-b6gg","author":"ubuntu","text":"SPECIFIC TEST CASES:
1. accTitle: 'Process Flow' → verify <title> element in SVG root
2. accDescr: 'Single line description' → verify <desc> element in SVG root
3. accDescr { multi-line } → verify multi-line <desc>
4. Both accTitle and accDescr → verify both elements present
5. Neither present → verify no empty <title>/<desc> elements
6. Works with flowchart, sequence, class, state → verify across diagram types","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:24Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-bfsm","title":"Implement classDef directive parsing and application","description":"# classDef Directive Support

## What mermaid-js Supports
classDef defines reusable CSS-like style classes that can be applied to nodes:
\`\`\`
classDef important fill:#f9f,stroke:#333,stroke-width:4px
classDef default fill:#fff
class nodeA,nodeB important
\`\`\`

Supported style properties: fill, stroke, stroke-width, stroke-dasharray, color, font-size, font-weight, font-family, background, border-radius, padding.

## Current State in frankenmermaid
- The \`class nodeA className\` syntax IS parsed (mermaid_parser.rs) and classes are stored on IrNode.classes
- The \`classDef\` syntax itself is NOT parsed — no style definitions are captured
- The SVG renderer does not apply any class-based styling to nodes

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser** (mermaid_parser.rs): Parse \`classDef className prop:value,prop:value\` lines. Store in a new \`IrStyleDef\` type in MermaidDiagramIr (add to fm-core).
2. **IR** (fm-core/src/lib.rs): Add \`style_defs: BTreeMap<String, IrStyleDef>\` to MermaidDiagramIr. IrStyleDef contains fill, stroke, stroke_width, stroke_dasharray, color, etc.
3. **SVG Renderer** (fm-render-svg/src/lib.rs): When rendering a node, look up its classes in style_defs and apply fill/stroke/etc. as inline SVG attributes.
4. **Terminal Renderer**: No visual impact (terminal doesn't support colors in basic mode).

## Test Plan
- Parse classDef with single property
- Parse classDef with multiple comma-separated properties
- Apply class to node and verify SVG attributes
- Verify default classDef applies to unclassed nodes
- Verify classDef with quoted values","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:43:18.353430251Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:23.643658982Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","parity","parser","render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-bfsm","depends_on_id":"bd-77gh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:22.387976601Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":139,"issue_id":"bd-bfsm","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

The parser implementation should handle classDef lines in the generic pre-processing phase (before diagram-type-specific parsing) so it works across all types. The IR's style_defs field is on MermaidDiagramIr which is shared by all diagram types.","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:23Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-bium","title":"Implement flowchart parallel edge syntax (A & B --> C & D)","description":"# Flowchart Parallel Edge Syntax

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
flowchart LR
  A & B --> C & D
\`\`\`
This creates 4 edges: A→C, A→D, B→C, B→D. The & operator allows declaring multiple source/target nodes in a single edge statement.

## Current State
- NOT parsed. The & character is not recognized in edge declarations.
- Mermaid parser's edge AST only supports single source → single target.

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: In parse_edge_statement_asts(), before looking for arrow operators, split node references on '&'. If either side contains '&', expand to multiple FlowAst::Edge entries (cartesian product of sources × targets).
2. **Tests**: A & B --> C (2 edges), A --> B & C (2 edges), A & B --> C & D (4 edges), single node (no &, unchanged behavior).

## Considerations
- The & operator is only valid in flowchart, not other diagram types
- Must handle whitespace: 'A&B' vs 'A & B' vs 'A &B'","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:14.744584229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:31.569465581Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","parity","parser"],"comments":[{"id":195,"issue_id":"bd-bium","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

 {"id":"bd-ckwu","title":"Sequence follow-up: layout/render participant group boxes from sequence_meta","description":"# Goal
Sequence `box ... end` groups are parsed into `IrSequenceMeta.participant_groups` but never laid out or rendered in the current pipeline, so the closed parity bead is not actually complete.

# Scope
- Convert `sequence_meta.participant_groups` into deterministic layout geometry in `fm-layout`.
- Render those group boxes across SVG, terminal, and canvas.
- Preserve deterministic ordering and nested-group behavior.
- Add focused tests proving `box` syntax produces visible output, not just parser metadata.

# Acceptance Criteria
- Sequence `box` groups appear in layout/render output across all three renderers.
- Geometry is deterministic and encloses the intended participant headers/lifelines.
- Focused parser/layout/render regression tests cover the feature.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-22T17:38:52.401186772Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T17:50:52.018035957Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T17:50:52.017538713Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","parity","render","sequence"]}

 {"id":"bd-ml2r.9.4","title":"[FNX] UX subtask: user guide, examples, and troubleshooting","description":"Goal
Ship a user-focused FNX integration guide with scenario-driven examples and decision playbooks.

Scope
- Add docs: when fnx helps, when to disable, and expected tradeoffs.
- Include before/after examples with command lines and output excerpts.
- Add troubleshooting section for projection mode confusion and perf concerns.

Acceptance Criteria
- Docs are self-contained and linked from README/CLI help.
- Examples are reproducible via scripts checked into repo.
- Documentation validation scripts run in CI.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements):
1) Unit tests
- Add comprehensive unit tests for happy path, edge cases, malformed input, and error paths for all touched modules.

2) Integration tests
- Add cross-crate integration coverage for touched parser/layout/render/CLI/WASM boundaries.

3) End-to-end tests
- Add or update reproducible E2E scripts that exercise user-visible flows affected by this bead.
- E2E scripts must run non-interactively and be CI-friendly.

4) Structured logging and evidence
- Emit machine-readable logs with at least these fields:
  `scenario_id`, `input_hash`, `fnx_mode`, `projection_mode`, `decision_mode`, `fnx_algorithm`, `parse_ms`, `analysis_ms`, `layout_ms`, `render_ms`, `diagnostic_count`, `fallback_reason`, `witness_hash`, `output_hash`, `pass_fail_reason`.
- Store artifacts/logs in deterministic, inspectable paths suitable for regression triage.

5) Determinism and regression checks
- Run repeated executions on representative scenarios (>=5 runs) and assert stable outputs/log summaries.
- Add explicit regression assertions for any metric or output changed by this bead.

6) Quality gates and build policy
- Required checks: `cargo check --workspace --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check`, and relevant `cargo test` scopes.
- All CPU-intensive cargo operations must be executed via `rch exec -- <cargo ...>` and captured in evidence notes/logs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:12.397625714Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T22:25:40.788369921Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","docs","fnx","ux","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.11.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:23:22.963912257Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:12.397625714Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:13.707759512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:13.769122960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:13.831272040Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-nep0","title":"EPIC: Full Mermaid-JS Feature Parity","description":"# EPIC: Full Mermaid-JS Feature Parity

## Goal
Achieve 100% feature parity with mermaid-js across ALL diagram types. Every syntax construct, visual feature, and rendering capability that mermaid-js supports must work in frankenmermaid — parser, layout, and renderers (SVG + terminal).

## Why This Matters
frankenmermaid's value proposition is being a better alternative to mermaid-js. But 'better' is meaningless if users hit unsupported syntax. Every 'Partial' in the support table is a potential user abandonment. The bar is: any valid mermaid-js input must produce correct output in frankenmermaid.

## Scope
This epic covers ALL 24+ diagram types. Each has a sub-epic with granular tasks for:
1. Parser completeness (every syntax construct)
2. Layout correctness (specialized algorithms where needed)
3. SVG rendering (every visual element)
4. Terminal rendering (best-effort representation)

## Success Criteria
- Every diagram type moves from 'Partial' to 'Implemented' in the support table
- The mermaid-js compatibility test suite (bd-2xl.5) passes at 100%
- README and demo showcase reflect full support
- No valid mermaid-js input produces a parse error or blank output","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:41:46.188949046Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T06:27:07.400334852Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-0pi0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:32.433199422Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-1a45","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:34.193057308Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-4gmf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:35.095329004Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-54qp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:25.218886499Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-68e5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:27.563546479Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-6qxv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:29.969622861Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-90m7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:24.432361365Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-b6gg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:32:21.231409785Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-ct6f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:28.362485999Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-f4er","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:31.592587609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nep0","depends_on_id":"bd-f6ow","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T  {"id":"bd-nep0.1","title":"Block Beta: add SVG family styling and hide placeholder space nodes","description":"# Block Beta SVG parity slice

Goal:
- Move Block Beta beyond generic node styling on the SVG surface.
- Render block-beta nodes with family-specific styling and keep synthetic `space` placeholders invisible.

Scope:
- Add explicit Block Beta SVG node classes.
- Apply family-specific fill/stroke/text styling.
- Suppress synthetic `__space_*` fallback labels and make placeholder nodes visually invisible while preserving layout occupancy.
- Add focused renderer tests.

Verification:
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p fm-render-svg block_beta -- --nocapture`
- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets`
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --check --package fm-render-svg`","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T06:13:12.607218730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T06:13:12.607218730Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["block-beta","parity","renderer","svg"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-nep0.1","depends_on_id":"bd-nep0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-23T06:13:12.607218730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-pdec","title":"Sequence parity: honor sequence.showSequenceNumbers init config","description":"Mermaid sequence diagrams support enabling message numbering through init/front-matter config via sequence.showSequenceNumbers. frankenmermaid already renders numbered messages when sequence_meta.autonumber is set, but the config path is not wired through. Add config parsing/storage and parser lowering so showSequenceNumbers enables autonumber deterministically, with focused tests for init JSON and front matter.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T19:05:16.952952248Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T19:08:25.315315840Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T19:08:25.314720413Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["config","parity","parser","render","sequence"]}
 {"id":"bd-peow","title":"Implement click directive rendering with URL targets","description":"# Click Directive Rendering

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
click nodeA href "https://example.com" "Tooltip text"
click nodeA callback "functionName"
click nodeA href "https://example.com" _blank
\`\`\`
Nodes become clickable links. URLs open in new tab, callbacks invoke JS functions.

## Current State
- Parser recognizes click directives and extracts href/callback values
- IrNode has \`href: Option<String>\` field that stores the URL
- SVG renderer does NOT wrap nodes in <a> elements or add cursor styling
- URL sanitization IS implemented (blocks javascript:, vbscript: schemes)

## Implementation Plan
1. **SVG Renderer**: When IrNode.href is Some, wrap the node's <g> element in an SVG <a> element with xlink:href. Add cursor:pointer CSS. Add <title> for tooltip.
2. **Tests**: Verify <a> wrapping in SVG output. Verify tooltip. Verify sanitized URLs don't produce <a> elements.

## Security
- URL sanitization already in place — must verify it covers all attack vectors
- No callback support (WASM/JS only) — document as intentional limitation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:43:18.616386903Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:13.933197674Z","closed_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:13.932777199Z","close_reason":"ALREADY IMPLEMENTED: SVG renderer at fm-render-svg/src/lib.rs lines 1671-1684 already wraps nodes in <a> elements with href, target=_blank, rel=noopener when IrNode.href is Some. Verified by code review. No work needed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","parity","render"],"comments":[{"id":141,"issue_id":"bd-peow","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-qj2b","title":"Implement state notes, guards, and transition actions","description":"# State Notes, Guards, and Actions

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
stateDiagram-v2
  state Active
  note right of Active: This state is active
  note left of Active
    Multi-line
    note content
  end note
  Active --> Done : complete [isValid] / cleanup()
  Active : entry: startTimer()
  Active : exit: stopTimer()
\`\`\`
- Notes: positioned left/right of states
- Guard conditions: [condition] on transitions
- Actions: / action() on transitions
- Entry/exit actions: declared as state descriptions

## Current State
- Notes: NOT parsed
- Guards/actions on transitions: NOT parsed (treated as part of label text)
- Entry/exit actions: NOT parsed

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Parse \`note right of\`/\`note left of\` statements. Parse guard conditions in brackets and actions after / in transition labels. Parse \`StateName : entry: action\` / \`exit: action\`.
2. **IR**: Add notes, guard/action fields to edges, entry/exit to nodes.
3. **SVG Renderer**: Draw note boxes, show guard/action text on edges.
4. **Tests**: Notes, guarded transitions, entry/exit actions.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:45:20.380064165Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:24.874643615Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","parser","render","state"],"comments":[{"id":154,"issue_id":"bd-qj2b","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

 {"id":"bd-qsr8","title":"Kanban: Implement card metadata, WIP limits, and priority styling","description":"# Kanban Rendering Improvements

## What mermaid-js Supports (v11+)
\`\`\`
kanban
  Todo
    id1[Design] @{ priority: high, assigned: alice }
    id2[Implement]
  In Progress @{ wip: 2 }
    id3[Testing]
  Done
    id4[Deploy]
\`\`\`
- Card metadata: priority, assigned, tags via @{} syntax
- Column WIP limits: @{ wip: N } on column headers
- Ticket base URL for linking cards to issue trackers
- Card styling based on priority/status

## Current State
- Columns and cards parsed
- @{} metadata syntax NOT parsed
- WIP limits NOT parsed or rendered
- No priority-based styling

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Parse @{key: value} syntax on cards and columns. Extract priority, assigned, tags, wip.
2. **IR**: Add metadata map to kanban card nodes and WIP limit to clusters.
3. **SVG Renderer**: Style cards by priority (high=red border, medium=yellow, low=green). Show WIP limit indicator on column headers. Show assigned name as badge.
4. **Tests**: Card metadata, WIP limits, priority styling.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:47:12.403662639Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:24.600150293Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["kanban","parity","parser","render"],"comments":[{"id":171,"issue_id":"bd-qsr8","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

 {"id":"bd-tvxc","title":"Class: Complete feature parity with mermaid-js","description":"# Class Diagram Feature Parity

## Current State
Class diagrams parse basic class declarations and relationships (~75% parser). The main gaps are member visibility modifiers, type annotations, generics, stereotypes, and namespace grouping.

## Gaps
1. Visibility modifiers (+, -, #, ~) on members/methods
2. Type annotations (returnType, parameterTypes)
3. Generic/template parameters (Class~T~)
4. Stereotypes (<<interface>>, <<abstract>>, <<enum>>)
5. Annotations and notes
6. Namespace grouping
7. Cardinality/multiplicity on relationship ends
8. Direction specification (class diagram direction)

## Reference
- mermaid-js docs: https://mermaid.js.org/syntax/classDiagram.html
- Parser: crates/fm-parser/src/mermaid_parser.rs lines 1108-1188","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:44:44.486148435Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:47.475904048Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["class","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-tvxc","depends_on_id":"bd-79sj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:47.474967185Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-tvxc","depends_on_id":"bd-9cy0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:46.634959240Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-tvxc","depends_on_id":"bd-y2qs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:45.810415633Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-u785","title":"EPIC: Killer Demo — Layout Regression Test Harness","description":"## Killer Demo — Layout Regression Test Harness

Build an automated visual regression testing harness that renders the complete FrankenMermaid test corpus to SVG/PNG, compares against golden reference images using perceptual diff, and produces a browsable HTML report. This serves dual purpose: regression detection AND an impressive demo of FrankenMermaid's rendering capabilities.

## Motivation
Layout algorithms are notoriously difficult to regression-test with unit tests alone. A pixel-level change in coordinate assignment can propagate to produce visually different (but structurally correct) layouts. Conversely, a bug might produce structurally incorrect output that looks "close enough" to pass manual review. The regression harness provides:
1. **Automated comparison:** perceptual diff between current and golden renders
2. **Browsable report:** HTML gallery showing before/after/diff for each test case
3. **Demo showcase:** the report itself IS the demo — browse all diagram types, see FrankenMermaid's quality
4. **Regression budget:** configure acceptable diff threshold (e.g., 0.1% pixel difference) per diagram type

## Perceptual Diff Algorithm
Use structural similarity index (SSIM) or perceptual hash (pHash) rather than pixel-exact comparison:
- **SSIM:** Compares luminance, contrast, and structure. Score from 0 to 1, where 1 = identical.
  SSIM(x,y) = [l(x,y)]^α * [c(x,y)]^β * [s(x,y)]^γ
  where l = luminance, c = contrast, s = structure comparison
- **pHash:** DCT-based perceptual hash. Hamming distance between hashes measures visual similarity.
- **Threshold:** SSIM > 0.99 = no regression, 0.95-0.99 = warning, < 0.95 = regression

## Implementation
1. Create `regression-harness/` binary crate in workspace.
2. Input: directory of .mmd files (test corpus) + golden reference renders.
3. Pipeline per file: parse → layout → render to SVG → rasterize to PNG (via resvg or similar).
4. Compare each PNG against golden reference using SSIM.
5. Generate HTML report: thumbnail grid, click to expand, before/after/diff overlay.
6. Golden reference update: `cargo run --bin regression-harness -- --update-goldens`.
7. CI integration: run on every PR that touches fm-layout or fm-render, fail if any SSIM < threshold.
8. Include timing data per render in the report (performance regression detection).

## Demo Aspect
The regression report doubles as the project's visual showcase:
- Categories: flowcharts, sequence diagrams, class diagrams, state diagrams, gantt charts, etc.
- Each category shows 5-10 representative diagrams at various complexities.
- Report includes metadata: node count, edge count, layout time, render time.
- Hosted version can be published to frankenmermaid.com/gallery for public demo.

## Success Metrics
- All diagram types in test corpus rendered and compared automatically
- SSIM threshold tuned to catch real regressions while ignoring sub-pixel jitter
- HTML report loads in < 2 seconds with 500+ thumbnails
- Golden update workflow is one command + explicit commit
- CI gate blocks merge on regression (SSIM < 0.95)
- Report generation completes in < 5 minutes for full corpus

## Dependencies
- Requires fm-render-svg (bd-1y5) for SVG output

 {"id":"bd-ulag","title":"Implement diagram-level title in IR and render across all diagram types","description":"# Diagram-Level Title Support

## What mermaid-js Supports
Many diagram types support a title directive:
\`\`\`
---
title: My Diagram Title
---
flowchart LR
  A --> B
\`\`\`
Or inline: \`title My Diagram\` in gantt, journey, timeline, pie, xychart, quadrant.

The title renders as centered text above the entire diagram.

## Current State
- YAML front-matter title: NOT extracted from init config
- Inline 'title' directive: Parsed and SKIPPED in gantt, journey, pie, etc.
- MermaidDiagramMeta has NO title field
- SVG renderer does NOT render any diagram title

## Implementation Plan
1. **IR** (fm-core): Add title: Option<String> to MermaidDiagramMeta.
2. **Parser**: In front-matter parsing, extract title field. In diagram-specific parsers (gantt, journey, timeline, pie, xychart, quadrant), capture the title directive text and store in meta.title.
3. **SVG Renderer**: When meta.title is Some, render as centered <text> element above the diagram content. Adjust viewBox to accommodate title height.
4. **Tests**: YAML front-matter title, inline title in gantt, inline title in pie, no title (verify no empty text element), long title (verify wrapping or truncation).

## Impact
This is a high-visibility feature — users expect their diagram title to show up. Currently it silently vanishes.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:52.006771142Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T06:27:08.143993131Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fm-core","parity","render"],"comments":[{"id":199,"issue_id":"bd-ulag","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)

 {"id":"bd-vb9","title":"EPIC: Advanced Layout Algorithms","description":"Upgrade fm-layout with superior layout algorithms that produce significantly better-looking diagrams than mermaid-js. The existing Sugiyama implementation is solid but we need: (1) dramatically improved cycle handling that doesn't just reverse edges but produces aesthetically pleasing layouts, (2) force-directed layout for organic/non-hierarchical diagrams, (3) tree layout for mindmaps/org charts, (4) specialized layouts for timeline/gantt/sankey/kanban/grid diagrams, (5) constraint-based refinement for fine-tuning. The layout engine is the single biggest factor in diagram quality -- this is where we win against mermaid-js. All algorithms must maintain the determinism guarantee: identical input always produces identical output.

## Success Criteria

- Every capability listed in this epic is delivered completely through child issues; no scope cuts and no loss of planned functionality.
- Child tasks include comprehensive unit/integration coverage and e2e coverage with detailed logging; test evidence is available and reproducible.
- Performance, determinism, and reliability expectations for this epic are validated with measurable checks.
- User-facing behavior is documented (README/docs/config/migration notes) for all newly shipped functionality.
- Release readiness is proven by passing workspace quality gates and epic-level validation flows end-to-end.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:31:28.678967346Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T01:49:47.883609448Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","layout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vb9","depends_on_id":"bd-3bc.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":42,"issue_id":"bd-vb9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Idea-wizard expansion: added bd-vb9.8 (layout time-budget guardrails + deterministic fallback) to protect reliability on pathological graphs.","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:46:27Z"}]}

-
-### CLI/User-Facing Claims
-
-Current CLI support reporting in [`crates/fm-cli/src/main.rs`](/data/projects/frankenmermaid/crates/fm-cli/src/main.rs) already contradicts any blanket "100% parity" claim:
-
-- `Flowchart`: `full`
-- `Sequence`, `Class`, `State`, `ER`: `partial`
-- `Pie`, `Gantt`, `Journey`, `Mindmap`, `Timeline`, `QuadrantChart`, `Requirement`, `PacketBeta`: `basic`
-- `GitGraph`, `Sankey`, `XYChart`, `BlockBeta`, `ArchitectureBeta`, all `C4*`: `unsupported`
-
-That is the current hard baseline until implementation and tests prove otherwise.
-
-## Highest-Value Gaps
-
-1. Parser parity for detected-but-fallback families:
-   `Sankey`, `XYChart`, `BlockBeta`, `ArchitectureBeta`, `C4*`
-2. CLI support metadata drift:
-   `GitGraph` has a dedicated parser but is still reported as unsupported
-3. Missing reference-spec documents:
-   no `EXISTING_*_STRUCTURE.md` or `PROPOSED_ARCHITECTURE.md` yet
-4. Missing conformance infrastructure:
-   no fixture-based parity harness against the FrankenTUI reference surfaces
-
-## Required Next Documents
-
-- `EXISTING_FRANKENTUI_MERMAID_STRUCTURE.md`
-- `PROPOSED_ARCHITECTURE.md`
-
-Those two documents are required before any honest claim of 100% parity.
-
-## Exit Condition For 100%
-
-This file can only move to "100% feature parity" when:
-
-- every in-scope legacy/reference feature is documented,
-- every documented feature is implemented in Rust,
-- every implementation is backed by conformance tests,
-- no row above remains `Partial`, `Fallback`, or `Missing`.
+| Shared IR pipeline | Complete | `MermaidDiagramIr` feeds all renderers |
+| Deterministic layout | Complete | BTreeMap everywhere, stable tie-breaking, 16 algorithms |
+| SVG renderer | Partial | 21 node shapes, gradients, shadows, themes, accessibility, pie/quadrant/xychart specializations |
+| Terminal renderer | Partial | 4 sub-cell modes, diff engine, minimap, glyphs |
+| Canvas/WASM | Partial | Canvas2D with mock context, viewport transforms |
+| Diff engine | Complete | Structural node/edge diffing with change classification |
+| Minimap | Complete | Density-aware scaling with viewport indicator |
+
+## Remaining Gaps vs FrankenTUI
+
+### Parser-Level
+
+- Click callbacks (`click nodeId call functionName`) — explicitly unsupported (warning emitted)
+- `classDef default` — neither FrankenTUI nor frankenmermaid supports this
+
+### Rendering-Level
+
+- Terminal pie chart rendering (wedge-by-pixel, legend) — SVG only
+- Terminal gantt chart rendering (task bars, date axis) — SVG only
+- Debug overlay panel (crossings, bends, symmetry metrics) — TUI-specific
+- Fidelity tier selection for terminal (Outline/Compact/Normal/Fine) — not applicable to SVG
+- Interactive selection state (node highlight, directional navigation) — TUI-specific
+
+### Areas Where frankenmermaid Is Ahead of FrankenTUI
+
+- Participant groups with color support
+- Lifecycle events (create/destroy)
+- Fragment alternatives with labeled sections
+- Fragment nesting (children tracking)
+- 16 dedicated layout algorithms (vs ~6 in FrankenTUI)
+- SVG gradients, shadows, glow effects
+- Canvas2D rendering backend
+- WASM/JavaScript API
+- 10 theme presets (vs 6 in FrankenTUI)
+- Accessibility (ARIA labels, accTitle/accDescr, keyboard nav)
